[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: cancelRoot] Can an unprivileged authorized operator, under the precondition that the maker has called setIsAuthorized(operator, true) on Midnight for any purpose (e.g., routing via MidnightBundles), call EcrecoverRatifier.cancelRoot(maker, root) to permanently freeze all of the maker's offers signed under that Merkle root, violating the invariant that offer cancellation requires explicit maker intent, causing scoped impact: all maker offers under the canceled root become permanently unfillable (temporary-to-permanent fund freeze for takers awaiting fills, and maker's liquidity provision is griefed)? Proof idea: deploy EcrecoverRatifier, authorize operator on Midnight, have operator call cancelRoot, then attempt take() with a valid proof and assert it reverts with RootCanceled().

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: cancelRoot + isAuthorized coarse-grain] Can an unprivileged authorized address (e.g., a smart-contract router that was authorized for repay/withdraw only), under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][router] == true and the router has no cancelRoot access control, call cancelRoot(maker, root) to freeze all maker offers under that root, violating the invariant that authorization is not scoped by action and that cancelRoot must only be callable by the maker or an explicitly trusted delegate, causing scoped impact: maker's entire offer book under the root is frozen without maker consent? Proof idea: unit test where a MidnightBundles-authorized address calls cancelRoot and assert isRootCanceled[maker][root] == true, then assert subsequent take() reverts.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: cancelRoot + setIsAuthorized chain] Can an unprivileged authorized address, under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][attacker] == true and Midnight.setIsAuthorized allows authorized addresses to authorize further addresses, call setIsAuthorized(attacker2, true, maker) and then have attacker2 call cancelRoot(maker, root), violating the invariant that authorization chains must not amplify cancelRoot access, causing scoped impact: maker's offers frozen via a multi-hop authorization chain the maker did not anticipate? Proof idea: fuzz authorization chain depth and assert cancelRoot is reachable from any authorized address.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + HashLib.offerTreeTypeHash] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a valid offer exists in a Merkle tree of height <= 20, submit ratifierData with proof.length == 21 to cause HashLib.offerTreeTypeHash(21) to revert with TreeTooHigh(), blocking the take() call and temporarily freezing the taker's ability to fill the offer, violating the invariant that a valid offer with a correct Merkle proof must always be fillable, causing scoped impact: taker's fill attempt is griefed by proof length manipulation? Proof idea: unit test with proof.length = 21 and assert take() reverts; confirm proof.length = 20 succeeds.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + HashLib.isLeaf] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a valid offer exists at leafIndex L in a tree of height H, submit leafIndex >= 2^H (e.g., leafIndex = 2^H) to cause HashLib.isLeaf to revert with LeafIndexOutOfRange(), blocking the take() and temporarily freezing the fill, violating the invariant that isRatified must revert gracefully without blocking valid fills, causing scoped impact: taker's fill is griefed by out-of-range leafIndex? Proof idea: fuzz leafIndex in [0, 2^proof.length + 10] and assert revert exactly when leafIndex >> proof.length != 0.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take consumed tracking] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a buy offer (offer.buy == true) has maxAssets > 0 and offer.tick is near MAX_TICK (price << WAD), repeatedly call take() with units = 1 such that buyerAssets = mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD) == 0, causing consumed[maker][group] to never increase, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity and that partial fills must update consumed state, causing scoped impact: the offer's maxAssets cap is never enforced, allowing the taker to fill the offer an unbounded number of times and extract unbounded credit units from the seller? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take consumed tracking] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a sell offer (offer.buy == false) has maxAssets > 0 and offer.tick == MAX_TICK (sellerPrice rounds to 0 via tickToPrice), repeatedly call take() with units = 1 such that sellerAssets = mulDivUp(1, 0, WAD) == 0, causing consumed[maker][group] to never increase, violating the invariant that consumed state must be updated on every partial fill, causing scoped impact: the sell offer's maxAssets cap is never enforced and the maker's debt obligation grows without bound? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take consumed tracking] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a buy offer has maxAssets = N and consumed[maker][group] = N - k where k < mulDivDown(1, buyerPrice, WAD), attempt a final fill with units = 1 causing newConsumed = N - k + buyerAssets > N and reverting with ConsumedAssets(), permanently locking the remaining k assets of offer capacity, violating the invariant that all offer capacity must be reachable, causing scoped impact: the last dust-sized slice of the offer is permanently frozen and the maker's offer is effectively under-filled? Proof idea: fuzz k in [1, buyerPrice/WAD - 1], fill offer to N - k, then attempt units = 1 fill and assert ConsumedAssets() revert.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take maturity boundary] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that offer.expiry == market.maturity and block.timestamp == market.maturity, call take() with sellerDebtIncrease > 0 (since block.timestamp <= market.maturity passes the CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity check), then at block.timestamp == market.maturity + 1 the same offer is expired (OfferExpired revert), violating the invariant that the maturity boundary must not allow debt increase by timestamp equality, causing scoped impact: a 1-second window at maturity allows debt-increasing fills that should be post-maturity-blocked, temporarily griefing the seller? Proof idea: warp to maturity, fill sell offer, assert debt increases; warp to maturity + 1, assert OfferExpired.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take maturity boundary] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that offer.start == market.maturity (a post-maturity offer) and block.timestamp == market.maturity, call take() with sellerDebtIncrease > 0 (since block.timestamp <= market.maturity), violating the invariant that debt must not increase after maturity, causing scoped impact: a post-maturity offer with start == maturity allows debt increase at the exact maturity timestamp, griefing the seller by creating debt they cannot repay post-maturity? Proof idea: create offer with start = maturity, warp to maturity, call take() with sell side, assert sellerDebtIncrease > 0 and CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity is NOT triggered.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take settlement fee at maturity boundary] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that block.timestamp == market.maturity (timeToMaturity = zeroFloorSub(maturity, maturity) = 0), observe that settlementFee uses the 0d breakpoint (settlementFeeCbp0 * CBP), and if settlementFeeCbp0 == 0 then buyerAssets == sellerAssets, causing claimableSettlementFee to not increase, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be collected on every take, causing scoped impact: fee collection is griefed at the maturity boundary when the 0d fee is zero? Proof idea: set settlementFeeCbp0 = 0, warp to maturity, call take(), assert claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] unchanged.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.supplyCollateral bitmap limit] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that the taker already has MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) activated collaterals and the offer's takerCallback tries to call supplyCollateral for a 17th collateral, cause the callback to revert with TooManyActivatedCollaterals(), which propagates to take() reverting with WrongSellCallbackReturnValue(), temporarily freezing the taker's ability to fill the offer, violating the invariant that a valid offer with a valid signature must be fillable, causing scoped impact: taker's fill is griefed by bitmap limit when using a callback-based collateral supply flow? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.supplyCollateral bitmap limit] Can an unprivileged attacker, under the precondition that a borrower has 15 activated collaterals and is near the health threshold, call supplyCollateral for a 16th collateral (filling the bitmap), then the borrower cannot add a 17th collateral to improve health, causing the borrower to become liquidatable, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable due to bitmap edge cases, causing scoped impact: borrower's funds are frozen/seized because the bitmap limit prevents health improvement? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take group budget exhaustion] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a maker has two offers in the same group (same offer.group bytes32) with different ticks (one cheap, one expensive), fill the cheap offer to exhaust consumed[maker][group] up to maxAssets, causing subsequent take() calls on the expensive offer to revert with ConsumedAssets(), temporarily freezing the expensive offer, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity but must not block other offers in the group, causing scoped impact: the maker's expensive offer is griefed by a taker filling the cheap offer first? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take group budget cross-market] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a maker has offers in the same group across two different markets (same offer.group, different market params), fill the offer in market A to exhaust consumed[maker][group], causing the offer in market B to be frozen, violating the invariant that group budget must only prevent total exposure exceeding budget but must not cause cross-market griefing, causing scoped impact: maker's offer in market B is temporarily frozen by a taker filling market A? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: cancelRoot + isRootCanceled immutability] Can an unprivileged authorized address, under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][attacker] == true, call cancelRoot(maker, root) to set isRootCanceled[maker][root] = true, and since there is no uncancelRoot function, permanently freeze all offers under that root, violating the invariant that temporary freezing must be reversible, causing scoped impact: maker's entire offer book under the root is permanently frozen with no recovery path? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + EIP-712 domain block.chainid] Can an unprivileged attacker, under the precondition that the chain undergoes a hard fork changing block.chainid, observe that the EcrecoverRatifier domain separator keccak256(abi.encode(EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH, block.chainid, address(this))) changes, invalidating all previously signed offer roots, while Midnight.sol uses INITIAL_CHAIN_ID for market IDs (so markets still exist), causing all EcrecoverRatifier-backed offers to revert with InvalidSignature() or Unauthorized(), violating the invariant that valid offers must remain fillable, causing scoped impact: all maker offers using EcrecoverRatifier are temporarily frozen post-fork until re-signed? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take partial fill with maxAssets and rounding] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that a buy offer has maxAssets = M and buyerPrice is such that mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD) has significant rounding loss, fill the offer in N partial fills each consuming floor(units * buyerPrice / WAD) assets, causing the total consumed to be less than M * N / units due to accumulated rounding, violating the invariant that consumed state must accurately track total filled assets, causing scoped impact: the offer is over-filled beyond its intended maxAssets due to rounding accumulation? Proof idea: fuzz units and buyerPrice, fill offer in many small increments, assert sum(buyerAssets per fill) < maxAssets but total units filled > maxAssets / buyerPrice * WAD.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take partial fill with maxUnits and dust] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that an offer has maxUnits = U and consumed[maker][group] = U - 1, attempt a final fill with units = 1 causing newConsumed = U which passes the ConsumedUnits check, but if units = 0 is passed instead, newConsumed = U - 1 still passes, allowing a no-op fill that calls isRatified and the callback without consuming the offer, violating the invariant that partial fills must update consumed state before any exploitable reentry point, causing scoped impact: callback is triggered without consuming the offer, potentially griefing the maker via repeated callback invocations? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take offer.expiry boundary] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that offer.expiry == block.timestamp (the offer expires in the current block), call take() and succeed (since block.timestamp <= offer.expiry), but a competing taker in the same block who submits after the first fill may find the offer partially consumed, violating the invariant that offer expiry must not create race conditions that freeze remaining capacity, causing scoped impact: remaining offer capacity is frozen for the duration of the block after expiry? Proof idea: set expiry = block.timestamp, fill partially, assert remaining capacity is inaccessible after expiry.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take offer.start boundary] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that offer.start == block.timestamp + 1 (offer not yet started), call take() and observe OfferNotStarted() revert, then at block.timestamp + 1 the offer becomes valid, but if the offer's root was canceled in the intervening block, the offer is permanently frozen, violating the invariant that offers must be fillable once their start time is reached, causing scoped impact: a time-locked offer is griefed by root cancellation in the window between signing and start? [File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take reduceOnly partial fill] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that offer.reduceOnly == true and offer.buy == true (maker is buyer, reduceOnly means buyerCreditIncrease must be 0), attempt a fill where the buyer has no existing debt (buyerPos.debt == 0), causing buyerCreditIncrease = units > 0 and the take to revert with MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased(), temporarily freezing the offer for any taker who cannot reduce the buyer's debt, violating the invariant that reduceOnly offers must be fillable when the maker has debt, causing scoped impact: the offer is frozen for all takers until the maker acquires debt? Proof idea: create reduceOnly buy offer, attempt fill with buyer debt = 0, assert MakerCreditOrDebtIncreased(); give buyer debt, retry and assert success.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take settlement fee rounding at breakpoint] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that timeToMaturity is exactly at a fee breakpoint (e.g., timeToMaturity == 7 days), observe that settlementFee = (feeLower * (end - 7d) + feeUpper * (7d - start)) / (end - start) = feeLower (since 7d - start = 0 for the 7d breakpoint), and if feeLower == feeUpper then the fee is constant across the breakpoint, but if feeLower > feeUpper the fee drops discontinuously, causing buyerAssets - sellerAssets to change by a large amount at the breakpoint, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be piecewise linear and continuous, causing scoped impact: takers are griefed by unexpected fee jumps at breakpoints? Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity around each breakpoint and assert fee continuity.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take settlement fee rounding truncation] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that feeLower and feeUpper are set such that (feeLower * (end - timeToMaturity) + feeUpper * (timeToMaturity - start)) is not divisible by (end - start), observe that the settlement fee is truncated (rounded down), causing buyerAssets - sellerAssets to be 1 wei less than expected, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be collected accurately, causing scoped impact: fee rounding leakage accumulates over many takes, griefing the fee claimer? Proof idea: fuzz timeToMaturity and fee values, assert sum(buyerAssets - sellerAssets) >= expected_fee * num_takes.

[File: 'src/ratifiers/EcrecoverRatifier.sol -> Scope: Medium Temporary freezing or griefing of user funds through dust, rounding, maturity boundary, bitmap limit, or partial-fill edge cases'] [Function: isRatified + Midnight.take bitmap limit at collateralIndex 127] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that the market has 128 collateral assets (MAX_COLLATERALS = 128) and the borrower activates collateral at index 127 (the highest), observe that setBit(bitmap, 127) sets bit 127 of the uint128 collateralBitmap, and if the borrower already has 15 other collaterals, countBits(newBitmap) = 16 which passes, but if they have 16, countBits = 17 which reverts with TooManyActivatedCollaterals(), temporarily freezing the borrower's ability to add collateral at index 127, violating the invariant that collateral bitmap/list state must match actual deposited collateral, causing scoped impact: borrower cannot add high-index collateral to maintain health? Proof idea: activate 16 collaterals at indices 0-15, attempt supplyCollateral at index 127, assert TooManyActivatedCollaterals().
